title: Find the final agreed version number in a thread with superseded proposals
instruction:
Read the thread with subject "Release Version for Q3 Launch" from the sender
Chen Wei. Identify the version number that both Chen Wei and Dana Okafor explicitly
agreed on in that thread. State the version as '4.2.1' (without a 'v' prefix).
Reply to Chen Wei's most recent message in the thread with exact text:
"Confirmed: version 4.2.1 is the agreed release."
eval_check_descriptions: Reply in correct thread with exact confirmation text
Reply is to Chen Wei's most recent message

emails.subject: RE: version decision
emails.body:
Just to confirm for everyone — we locked in v4.3.0 yesterday per Marcus's suggestion. Please update your branches.

No further discussion needed on this one.

Thanks,
Marcus

emails.subject: Reminder on document review timing
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Elena

emails.subject: Release Version for Q3 Launch
emails.body:
Hey all, just catching up on this thread.

Can we do v4.3.0 instead? More headroom for the SDK bump we discussed Friday. I think 4.2.x will be tight once the partner integrations land.

Thanks,
Marcus

emails.subject: Notes from the partner sync
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Release Version for Q3 Launch
emails.body:
v4.2.1 works for me, locked in. I'll update the release manifest.

Thanks,
Theo

emails.subject: Revised budget worksheet
emails.body:
I pulled the loose ends into one note for easier review.

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. If you want, I can turn this into a cleaner summary before the meeting.

Thanks,
Priya

emails.subject: Release Version for Q3 Launch
emails.body:
Agree on 4.2.x. Theo, good point about the payments module.

With the auth hotfix that landed this morning, let's make it v4.2.1 to capture that patch.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
Sharing the latest notes before tomorrow's check-in.

Most of the edits were minor, with one budget line still waiting on approval. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Marcus

emails.subject: Q3 Release Planning - Version Discussion
emails.body:
For the secondary product line, we're looking at either v4.2.1 or v5.0.0.

The v4.2.1 path keeps backward compatibility but limits the new API surface. v5.0.0 is a clean break. Thoughts?

Thanks,
Maya

emails.subject: Release Version for Q3 Launch
emails.body:
Thanks Avery, but v4.2.0 makes more sense given the feature delta. We merged the payments module last week and that bumps us past 4.1.x.

I'd rather not ship a 4.1.x release that immediately needs a follow-up.

Thanks,
Theo

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

The team is aligned on the main path and only needs confirmation on the follow-up items. Happy to consolidate comments if more feedback comes in.

Thanks,
Marcus

emails.subject: Release Version for Q3 Launch
emails.body:
Team, we need to lock in the release version for the Q3 launch.

I'm proposing we go with v4.1.0 as the base. It includes the core feature set and the stability fixes from the last sprint.

Let me know your thoughts before end of week.

Thanks,
Avery

emails.subject: Customer feedback from pilot cohort
emails.body:
Passing along the current draft so you have it in one place.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.

Thanks,
Jordan

emails.subject: Follow-up on venue estimate
emails.body:
Sending a quick status update from today's working session.

The open items are mostly ownership questions rather than scope changes. No response needed unless you want anything reordered.
